semanticCore: Exhausting wine, bleeding, draining dry, losing head, or siphon
primaryMeaning: Draining dry or losing intellect/exhausting source
quranicRelevance: Paradise wine not exhausting intellect/source (Yunzafoon - 'la yunzafoona', 'la... yunzafoon').

keyDerivations.meaning: To drain dry, exhaust supply, deplete wine, lose intellect, get drunk, or bleed

figurativeOrRareUsages: يُطَافُ عَلَيْهِمْ بِكَأْسٍ مِنْ مَعِينٍ ﴿٤٥﴾ ... لَا فِيهَا غَوْلٌ وَلَا هُمْ عَنْهَا يُنْزَفُونَ (37:45-47): Round them will be passed a cup of pure wine... No headiness/harm is in it, nor are they drained/deprived of intellect thereby.
discardedNoise: Nosebleed blood drainage (Nazf) in medical books.
Draining water wells dry with buckets.
summaryEn: The root represents exhaustion of wine and intellect, promising that Paradise beverages will never cause intoxication, mental depletion (Inzaf), or run dry.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر استنزاف مياه الآبار ونفاد الخمر وذهاب عقل الشارب (نزف)، واعداً بسلامة خمر الجنة فلا يزول معها عقل شاربها ولا تنفد أبداً.
rawText: مَنْزُوفٌ Exhausted: see an ex. voce ضَرَطَ. نَزَفَ 1 He entirely exhausted (S, Msb, K) a well, (Msb,) or the water of a well. (S, K.)
